blocks.value: An IRT item is described by up to three parameters. b (difficulty) shifts the curve left or right — higher b means a harder item. a (discrimination) controls the steepness — a steeper curve separates abilities more sharply. c (guessing) is the lower asymptote — the chance a very low-ability person still gets it right.

blocks.caption: Shifting b moves the curve: easy items sit left, hard items sit right.

items.q: A higher b parameter means the item is...
The a parameter controls the curve's...

items.back: Where the curve crosses P=0.5; higher = harder.
Steepness of the curve; higher = separates abilities more sharply.
Lower asymptote; chance a low-ability person answers correctly.
Uses all three parameters: a, b and c.

blocks.value: b sets difficulty, a sets discrimination (steepness), and c sets the guessing floor. The 3PL model combines all three.
